title: Refactor OCP-002 (OCP): Switch statement on token ID requires modification to handle new token types

detected_issue.description: Switch statement on token ID requires modification to handle new token types
detected_issue.reasoning: The getStringCharacters method uses a switch statement based on token IDs. Adding new token types would require modifying this switch block, violating the Open/Closed Principle.

fix_logic.summary: Replaced switch statement in getStringCharacters with a static lookup map to adhere to OCP.
fix_logic.rationale: The original `getStringCharacters` method used a switch statement on `JsonToken.id()`, which violates the Open/Closed Principle because adding new token types would require modifying this method. By introducing a static `Map` of `Function`s, the method itself becomes closed for modification. New token-specific character retrieval logic can be added by extending the `CHARACTER_PROVIDERS` map in its static initializer block, thus adhering to OCP. This approach keeps the logic localized within the parser where it has access to necessary internal state (`_tokenIncomplete`, `_finishString`, `_textBuffer`, `currentNameInBuffer`).
